data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260512T023608-03dd1cd6/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260512T023608-03dd1cd6/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260512T023608-03dd1cd6/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260512T023608-03dd1cd6/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260512T023608-03dd1cd6/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-42741260">
    <title>agent/chaos: cover concurrent-drain landing under shared-main contention</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
  After sister bead ddx-1f774edf removes network I/O from Land(), the
  drain becomes safe for external parallelism (multiple ddx work --once
  shells running concurrently). However, parallel workers will land their
  own bead's worktree against the same local main and may produce
  overlapping changes — multi-worker conflict frequency on Land()'s
  git merge step is unmeasured. The 2026-05-09 plan-review opus
  self-review identified this as a key risk: under high parallelism,
  preservation (resource_exhausted to iteration ref) could become the
  common path rather than the exception, defeating the gain.

  This bead adds chaos coverage for the parallel-landing case and a
  sentinel that fails if the preservation rate exceeds an acceptable
  threshold under simulated load.

CURRENT STATE
  - cli/internal/bead/chaos_test.go and chaos2_test.go cover
    bead.Store-layer concurrency only.
  - cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock_test.go covers single-worker
    semantics.
  - Sister bead ddx-c019e48a covers stale-lock recovery for a single
    worker's local-only land. It does not cover N>1 workers landing in
    parallel.
  - No test verifies what happens when worker A lands branch-A into
    main, then worker B (whose worktree was branched from pre-A main)
    tries to land branch-B with overlapping changes.

PROPOSED FIX
  Add three chaos tests in cli/internal/agent/landing_chaos_test.go (new
  file, or extend tracker_lock_test.go):

  1. TestConcurrentLand_NonOverlappingChanges_BothLandClean:
     - Spawn 3 worker goroutines, each with its own bead/worktree
       touching disjoint files.
     - All three call Land() concurrently.
     - Assert: all three land on local main, in some order; no
       preservation; main contains all three commits.

  2. TestConcurrentLand_OverlappingChanges_OneLandsRestPreserve:
     - Spawn 3 worker goroutines, each touching the same file with
       different content.
     - All three call Land() concurrently.
     - Assert: exactly one lands; the other two are preserved to
       iteration refs; the preserved branches are intact and contain
       their bead's full work.

  3. TestConcurrentLand_PreservationRateUnderLoadIsBounded:
     - Spawn N worker goroutines (N=4) over M cycles (M=10), each with
       a randomized mix of overlapping/non-overlapping changes.
     - Assert: preservation rate is < 50% (i.e., at least half of
       attempts land cleanly). This is a smoke check that parallel
       drain is viable, not a hard SLA.

  Use deterministic timing (channels or synctest), not wall-clock.
  Use testutils.NewFixtureRepo or t.TempDir() per test.
  Depends on sister bead ddx-1f774edf so the lock holds are sub-second
  (otherwise the test exercises today's contention bug, not the
  parallel-landing concern).

NON-SCOPE
  - Multi-process chaos (these are goroutine-level concurrent landings
    using fake LandingGitOps that mirror the real merge logic).
  - Coordinating workers via a "land queue" abstraction (separate
    architectural concern; this bead establishes the baseline measurement
    against which any such work would be evaluated).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead ddx-1f774edf (depends on it landing first).
  - Sister bead (just-filed) "agent/lock: instrument bead.Store
    WithLock wait time" — its metrics are referenced by these tests'
    assertions if available.
  - Sister bead ddx-c019e48a complements this for the single-worker
    stale-lock case.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. cli/internal/agent/landing_chaos_test.go (new file or extending tracker_lock_test.go) contains TestConcurrentLand_NonOverlappingChanges_BothLandClean asserting all parallel non-conflicting lands succeed.
2. The same file contains TestConcurrentLand_OverlappingChanges_OneLandsRestPreserve asserting exactly one of three conflicting lands succeeds; the other two preserve correctly.
3. The same file contains TestConcurrentLand_PreservationRateUnderLoadIsBounded with N=4 workers over M=10 cycles, asserting preservation rate < 50%.
4. Tests use deterministic timing (channels or synctest) and per-test sandbox via testutils.NewFixtureRepo or t.TempDir().
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestConcurrentLand_' green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:6, area:agent, area:lock, kind:test, prevention</labels>
    <metadata base-rev="dbd00a1596f8e820b74a1f8883f2b60860f2258f" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260512T023608-03dd1cd6"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>
